headlines.title: U.S. Gasoline Prices Rise for First Time Since May as Iran Ceasefire Collapses
headlines.summary: U.S. gasoline prices have increased for the first time since May, with the sudden reversal directly tied to the collapse of the tentative ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran. headlines.title: Oil Jumps Above $87 as Russia-Ukraine Black Sea Attacks Escalate

headlines.title: Pakistan Races for Emergency LNG as Strait of Hormuz Traffic Stalls
headlines.summary: Pakistan is on track to procure the most LNG cargoes on the spot market since the Iran war began as the re-escalation in the Strait of Hormuz threatens to disrupt anew term supplies from Qatar. headlines.title: Iran Says It Will Continue Exporting Oil Despite Canceled U.S. Waiver
headlines.summary: Iran continues to export its oil despite last week’s cancellation of the U.S. waiver of its oil sales, Iranian Oil Minister, Mohsen Paknejad, said on Tuesday, as Tehran remains defiant and determined to control the Strait of Hormuz amid renewed hostilities and the reinstated U.S.

headlines.title: Asian Oil Importers Pivot to U.S. Supplies as Hormuz Risks Intensify
headlines.summary: Asian buyers are back to negotiating spot cargoes of U.S. crude oil after the expected recovery of flows through the Strait of Hormuz ground to a halt early this week with the latest re-escalation in the U.S.-Iran tensions. headlines.title: China Cuts Saudi Crude Orders as Hormuz Risks and Discounts Reshape Trade

headlines.title: Brent Surges Above $86 After Iran Strikes Two UAE Tankers in Hormuz
headlines.summary: Brent Crude prices soared by 3% early on Tuesday to above $86 per barrel, following an Iranian attack on two tankers of the United Arab Emirates in the southern lane of the Strait of Hormuz, in one of the biggest attacks in the recent re-escalation in the region. headlines.summary: Iran is estimated to have sneaked supertankers with a total of 12 million barrels of crude oil in the week between the end of the U.S. waiver on Iranian oil sales on July 7 and July 14, just as the U.S. announced the renewed blockade on Iranian ports and oil cargoes.
